model_narrative: We plan cement production and capacity expansion for a country with several plant sites and several market regions over a multi-period horizon. For each period we decide how intensely to run each production process at each plant, how much capacity of each productive unit to add at each plant, and for the units with economies of scale which discrete kiln size to install. We also decide how much finished product to ship from each plant to each market, how much to import or export, and how much intermediate material to import. A set of accounting variables tracks the investment, operating, transport, import, export, and working-capital cost of each period, and these are combined into a single discounted total. The objective is to minimize that total discounted cost over the whole horizon.

sets.name: I
sets.doc: the candidate plant sites where production capacity can exist or be installed

sets.name: J
sets.doc: the market regions where finished product demand must be met

sets.name: M
sets.doc: the productive units (kiln and milling technologies) that consume capacity

sets.name: P
sets.doc: the production processes that can be operated at a plant

sets.name: C
sets.doc: the commodities tracked in the material-balance accounting

sets.name: CF
sets.doc: the subset of commodities that are saleable final products; here just cement

sets.name: CI
sets.doc: the subset of commodities that are intermediates which may be imported

sets.name: T
sets.doc: the planning time periods over the horizon, in chronological order

sets.name: TE
sets.doc: the periods in which capacity expansion can be decided; the first period 1983-85 is excluded because existing capacity is already in place

sets.name: S
sets.doc: the discrete kiln sizes that an investment can take

sets.name: MS
sets.doc: the subset of productive units that exhibit economies of scale, so their capacity is added in discrete kiln sizes

sets.name: MP
sets.doc: the subset of productive units without economies of scale, whose capacity is added continuously

params.name: a
params.doc: input-output coefficient: tons of a commodity produced (positive) or consumed (negative) per unit level of running a process

params.name: b
params.doc: units of a productive unit's capacity consumed per unit level of running a process

params.name: k
params.doc: the existing base capacity of a productive unit at a plant at the start of the horizon, in tons per year

params.name: ts
params.doc: a 0/1 time-summation incidence: 1 if an expansion period is on or before a given period and therefore already contributing capacity, 0 otherwise

params.name: klim
params.doc: the maximum total capacity that may exist at a plant, in tons per year

params.name: rc
params.doc: the recurrent operating cost per unit level of a process

params.name: fc
params.doc: the fixed operating cost per ton of installed capacity of a productive unit

params.name: sigma
params.doc: the capital recovery factor that annualizes investment outlays; a scalar

params.name: delta
params.doc: the discount factor applied to each period's cost when forming the present value

params.name: site
params.doc: a location cost multiplier applied to investment outlays at a plant

params.name: size
params.doc: the capacity added by installing one kiln of a given discrete size, in tons per year

params.name: inv_cost
params.doc: the reference investment cost of a productive unit at its reference size

params.name: inv_size
params.doc: the reference size of a productive unit used to scale its investment cost

params.name: inv_scale
params.doc: the scale-economy exponent for a productive unit; together with the size and reference size it gives a constant unit investment cost for each discrete kiln size

params.name: muf
params.doc: the unit cost of shipping finished product from a plant to a market

params.name: muv
params.doc: the unit cost of delivering imported finished product to a market

params.name: mue
params.doc: the unit cost of moving finished product from a plant to the export point

params.name: mui
params.doc: the unit cost of moving an imported intermediate to a plant

params.name: ebu
params.doc: the maximum total finished-product export allowed in a period

params.name: vbu
params.doc: the maximum total finished-product import allowed in a period

params.name: vibu
params.doc: the maximum total clinker import allowed in a period

params.name: pv
params.doc: the import price of a final product or intermediate commodity

params.name: d
params.doc: the required demand for a final product in a market and period, in tons

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the operating level of a process at a plant in a period

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the quantity of a final product shipped from a plant to a market in a period

vars.name: v
vars.doc: the quantity of a final product imported and delivered to a market in a period

vars.name: vi
vars.doc: the quantity of an intermediate commodity imported to a plant in a period

vars.name: e
vars.doc: the quantity of a final product exported from a plant in a period

vars.name: h
vars.doc: the amount of capacity of a productive unit added at a plant in an expansion period, in tons per year

vars.name: y
vars.doc: equals 1 if a kiln of a given discrete size is installed for a scale-economy unit at a plant in an expansion period, and 0 otherwise

vars.name: phi
vars.doc: the total discounted cost over the horizon, which the model minimizes

vars.name: phikap
vars.doc: the capital-investment cost charged in a period

vars.name: phipsi
vars.doc: the recurrent operating cost charged in a period

vars.name: philam
vars.doc: the transport cost charged in a period

vars.name: phipi
vars.doc: the import cost charged in a period

vars.name: phieps
vars.doc: the export revenue earned in a period

vars.name: phiw
vars.doc: the working-capital cost charged in a period

constraints.name: mb
constraints.intent: For each commodity, plant, and period, production must be able to cover what leaves. The amount of the commodity produced by running the processes at the plant, together with any of that commodity imported when it is an intermediate, must be at least the amount of it that is shipped out to markets and exported when it is a final product.

constraints.name: cc
constraints.intent: For each productive unit, plant, and period, the capacity used cannot exceed the capacity available. The capacity consumed by running the processes at the plant must not be more than the existing base capacity of that unit plus all the capacity already added through expansions that are in effect by that period.

constraints.name: id
constraints.intent: For each scale-economy unit, plant, and expansion period, the capacity added must match the kiln chosen. The amount of capacity installed equals the capacity contributed by whichever discrete kiln size is selected in that decision.

constraints.name: ich
constraints.intent: For each scale-economy unit, plant, and expansion period, at most one kiln size may be chosen. The selection across all available sizes can pick at most a single size.

constraints.name: limit
constraints.intent: For each plant, the total capacity that ever exists across the scale-economy units cannot exceed the plant's capacity ceiling. Summing each such unit's existing base capacity and all the capacity it adds over the horizon must stay within the plant's limit.

constraints.name: mr
constraints.intent: For each final product, market, and period, supply must meet demand. The total shipped to the market from all plants plus what is imported to that market must be at least the required demand there.

constraints.name: eb
constraints.intent: For each period, total exports of final products are capped. The sum of all final-product exports from every plant must not exceed the export limit for that period.

constraints.name: vb
constraints.intent: For each period, total imports of final products are capped. The sum of all final-product imports delivered to every market must not exceed the import limit for that period.

constraints.name: vib
constraints.intent: For each period, total clinker imports are capped. The sum of clinker imported to every plant must not exceed the clinker import limit for that period.

constraints.name: obj_def
constraints.intent: The total discounted cost is defined by adding up, over all periods, the discounted sum of that period's investment, operating, transport, import, and working-capital costs less its export revenue.

constraints.name: apsi
constraints.intent: For each period, the recurrent operating cost is defined. It is a small scaling of the combined process operating cost across all plants and the fixed cost of all installed capacity, where installed capacity means each unit's base capacity plus the expansions in effect by that period.

constraints.name: akap
constraints.intent: For each period, the capital-investment cost is defined. It is the capital recovery factor applied to the location-adjusted investment outlays charged in that period: for the scale-economy units, the cost of each chosen kiln size, and for the units without scale economies, the cost proportional to the capacity added, counting only the expansions that are in effect by that period.

constraints.name: alam
constraints.intent: For each period, the transport cost is defined. It is a small scaling of the cost of shipping final products from plants to markets, delivering imported final products to markets, moving exported final products to the export point, and moving imported intermediates to plants.

constraints.name: aeps
constraints.intent: For each period, the export revenue is defined as a small scaling of the total quantity of final products exported from all plants in that period.

constraints.name: api
constraints.intent: For each period, the import cost is defined. It is a small scaling of the cost of imported final products delivered to markets plus the cost of imported intermediates delivered to plants, each valued at its import price.

constraints.name: aw
constraints.intent: For each period, the working-capital cost is defined as a fixed fraction of the sum of that period's recurrent operating cost and its import cost.

query: Engineering is floating the idea of committing to wet-kiln technology somewhere in the network rather than going all-dry. There's a proposal on the table to require at least one wet-kiln to be built at some plant over the whole horizon — put that in and tell me the resulting total cost.
expected_pyomo: model.commit_wet = Constraint(expr=sum(model.y['wet-kiln', i, s, te] for i in model.I for s in model.S for te in model.TE) >= 1)

query: We're looking at a policy to curb the country's reliance on imported cement at the Hodeideh port. The idea is to hold the cement brought in through Hodeideh, summed across all four planning periods, to no more than fifteen hundred units. Layer that ceiling in and tell me where total cost lands.
expected_pyomo: model.cap_hodeideh_imports = Constraint(expr=sum(model.v['cement', 'hodeideh', t] for t in model.T) <= 1500)

query: Regional development is pushing for Baijil to get a cement plant at some point over the horizon. Require at least one investment there and tell me the impact on total cost.
expected_pyomo: model.invest_baijil = Constraint(expr=sum(model.y[ms, 'baijil', s, te] for ms in model.MS for s in model.S for te in model.TE) >= 1)

sets.name: T
sets.doc: the planning time periods over the horizon, in chronological order

sets.name: TE
sets.doc: the periods in which capacity expansion can be decided; the first period 1983-85 is excluded because existing capacity is already in place

sets.name: S
sets.doc: the discrete kiln sizes that an investment can take

sets.name: MS
sets.doc: the subset of productive units that exhibit economies of scale, so their capacity is added in discrete kiln sizes

sets.name: MP
sets.doc: the subset of productive units without economies of scale, whose capacity is added continuously

params.name: a
params.doc: input-output coefficient: tons of a commodity produced (positive) or consumed (negative) per unit level of running a process

params.name: b
params.doc: units of a productive unit's capacity consumed per unit level of running a process

params.name: k
params.doc: the existing base capacity of a productive unit at a plant at the start of the horizon, in tons per year

params.name: ts
params.doc: a 0/1 time-summation incidence: 1 if an expansion period is on or before a given period and therefore already contributing capacity, 0 otherwise

params.name: klim
params.doc: the maximum total capacity that may exist at a plant, in tons per year

params.name: rc
params.doc: the recurrent operating cost per unit level of a process

params.name: fc
params.doc: the fixed operating cost per ton of installed capacity of a productive unit

params.name: sigma
params.doc: the capital recovery factor that annualizes investment outlays; a scalar

params.name: delta
params.doc: the discount factor applied to each period's cost when forming the present value

params.name: site
params.doc: a location cost multiplier applied to investment outlays at a plant

params.name: size
params.doc: the capacity added by installing one kiln of a given discrete size, in tons per year

params.name: inv_cost
params.doc: the reference investment cost of a productive unit at its reference size

params.name: inv_size
params.doc: the reference size of a productive unit used to scale its investment cost

params.name: inv_scale
params.doc: the scale-economy exponent for a productive unit; together with the size and reference size it gives a constant unit investment cost for each discrete kiln size

params.name: muf
params.doc: the unit cost of shipping finished product from a plant to a market

params.name: muv
params.doc: the unit cost of delivering imported finished product to a market

params.name: mue
params.doc: the unit cost of moving finished product from a plant to the export point

params.name: mui
params.doc: the unit cost of moving an imported intermediate to a plant

params.name: ebu
params.doc: the maximum total finished-product export allowed in a period

params.name: vbu
params.doc: the maximum total finished-product import allowed in a period

params.name: vibu
params.doc: the maximum total clinker import allowed in a period

params.name: pv
params.doc: the import price of a final product or intermediate commodity

params.name: d
params.doc: the required demand for a final product in a market and period, in tons

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the operating level of a process at a plant in a period

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the quantity of a final product shipped from a plant to a market in a period

vars.name: v
vars.doc: the quantity of a final product imported and delivered to a market in a period

vars.name: vi
vars.doc: the quantity of an intermediate commodity imported to a plant in a period

vars.name: e
vars.doc: the quantity of a final product exported from a plant in a period

vars.name: h
vars.doc: the amount of capacity of a productive unit added at a plant in an expansion period, in tons per year

vars.name: y
vars.doc: equals 1 if a kiln of a given discrete size is installed for a scale-economy unit at a plant in an expansion period, and 0 otherwise

vars.name: phi
vars.doc: the total discounted cost over the horizon, which the model minimizes

vars.name: phikap
vars.doc: the capital-investment cost charged in a period

vars.name: phipsi
vars.doc: the recurrent operating cost charged in a period

vars.name: philam
vars.doc: the transport cost charged in a period

vars.name: phipi
vars.doc: the import cost charged in a period

vars.name: phieps
vars.doc: the export revenue earned in a period

vars.name: phiw
vars.doc: the working-capital cost charged in a period

constraints.name: mb
constraints.intent: For each commodity, plant, and period, production must be able to cover what leaves. The amount of the commodity produced by running the processes at the plant, together with any of that commodity imported when it is an intermediate, must be at least the amount of it that is shipped out to markets and exported when it is a final product.

constraints.name: cc
constraints.intent: For each productive unit, plant, and period, the capacity used cannot exceed the capacity available. The capacity consumed by running the processes at the plant must not be more than the existing base capacity of that unit plus all the capacity already added through expansions that are in effect by that period.

constraints.name: id
constraints.intent: For each scale-economy unit, plant, and expansion period, the capacity added must match the kiln chosen. The amount of capacity installed equals the capacity contributed by whichever discrete kiln size is selected in that decision.

constraints.name: ich
constraints.intent: For each scale-economy unit, plant, and expansion period, at most one kiln size may be chosen. The selection across all available sizes can pick at most a single size.

constraints.name: limit
constraints.intent: For each plant, the total capacity that ever exists across the scale-economy units cannot exceed the plant's capacity ceiling. Summing each such unit's existing base capacity and all the capacity it adds over the horizon must stay within the plant's limit.

constraints.name: mr
constraints.intent: For each final product, market, and period, supply must meet demand. The total shipped to the market from all plants plus what is imported to that market must be at least the required demand there.

constraints.name: eb
constraints.intent: For each period, total exports of final products are capped. The sum of all final-product exports from every plant must not exceed the export limit for that period.

constraints.name: vb
constraints.intent: For each period, total imports of final products are capped. The sum of all final-product imports delivered to every market must not exceed the import limit for that period.

constraints.name: vib
constraints.intent: For each period, total clinker imports are capped. The sum of clinker imported to every plant must not exceed the clinker import limit for that period.

constraints.name: obj_def
constraints.intent: The total discounted cost is defined by adding up, over all periods, the discounted sum of that period's investment, operating, transport, import, and working-capital costs less its export revenue.

constraints.name: apsi
constraints.intent: For each period, the recurrent operating cost is defined. It is a small scaling of the combined process operating cost across all plants and the fixed cost of all installed capacity, where installed capacity means each unit's base capacity plus the expansions in effect by that period.

constraints.name: akap
constraints.intent: For each period, the capital-investment cost is defined. It is the capital recovery factor applied to the location-adjusted investment outlays charged in that period: for the scale-economy units, the cost of each chosen kiln size, and for the units without scale economies, the cost proportional to the capacity added, counting only the expansions that are in effect by that period.

constraints.name: alam
constraints.intent: For each period, the transport cost is defined. It is a small scaling of the cost of shipping final products from plants to markets, delivering imported final products to markets, moving exported final products to the export point, and moving imported intermediates to plants.

constraints.name: aeps
constraints.intent: For each period, the export revenue is defined as a small scaling of the total quantity of final products exported from all plants in that period.

constraints.name: api
constraints.intent: For each period, the import cost is defined. It is a small scaling of the cost of imported final products delivered to markets plus the cost of imported intermediates delivered to plants, each valued at its import price.

constraints.name: aw
constraints.intent: For each period, the working-capital cost is defined as a fixed fraction of the sum of that period's recurrent operating cost and its import cost.

query: Trade is looking at an export commitment — at least 200 units of cement shipped abroad over the horizon. Layer that in and tell me what it does to total cost.
expected_pyomo: model.min_export = Constraint(expr=sum(model.e[cf, i, t] for cf in model.CF for i in model.I for t in model.T) >= 200)

model_narrative: We plan cement production and capacity expansion for a country with several plant sites and several market regions over a multi-period horizon. For each period we decide how intensely to run each production process at each plant, how much capacity of each productive unit to add at each plant, and for the units with economies of scale which discrete kiln size to install. We also decide how much finished product to ship from each plant to each market, how much to import or export, and how much intermediate material to import. A set of accounting variables tracks the investment, operating, transport, import, export, and working-capital cost of each period, and these are combined into a single discounted total. The objective is to minimize that total discounted cost over the whole horizon.

sets.name: I
sets.doc: the candidate plant sites where production capacity can exist or be installed

sets.name: J
sets.doc: the market regions where finished product demand must be met

sets.name: M
sets.doc: the productive units (kiln and milling technologies) that consume capacity

sets.name: P
sets.doc: the production processes that can be operated at a plant

sets.name: C
sets.doc: the commodities tracked in the material-balance accounting

sets.name: CF
sets.doc: the subset of commodities that are saleable final products; here just cement

sets.name: CI
sets.doc: the subset of commodities that are intermediates which may be imported

sets.name: T
sets.doc: the planning time periods over the horizon, in chronological order

sets.name: TE
sets.doc: the periods in which capacity expansion can be decided; the first period 1983-85 is excluded because existing capacity is already in place

sets.name: S
sets.doc: the discrete kiln sizes that an investment can take

sets.name: MS
sets.doc: the subset of productive units that exhibit economies of scale, so their capacity is added in discrete kiln sizes

sets.name: MP
sets.doc: the subset of productive units without economies of scale, whose capacity is added continuously

query: It bugs me that the plan leans so hard on Amran while Baijil sits underused — Amran ends up shipping far more cement than Baijil does over the horizon. Shouldn't Baijil at least be pulling its weight, putting out as much cement as Amran across the whole plan? What's the model seeing that keeps it from leveling those two up?
expected_pyomo: model.baijil_ge_amran = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x['cement', 'baijil', j, t] for j in model.J for t in model.T) >= sum(model.x['cement', 'amran', j, t] for j in model.J for t in model.T))

query: I keep seeing big slugs of imported cement landing in a single period when the import bill spikes. Couldn't we just smooth that out and hold nationwide cement imports to no more than six hundred units in every single planning period? What's keeping the plan from spreading the buying more evenly?

params.doc: the maximum total capacity that may exist at a plant, in tons per year

params.name: rc
params.doc: the recurrent operating cost per unit level of a process

params.name: fc
params.doc: the fixed operating cost per ton of installed capacity of a productive unit

params.name: sigma
params.doc: the capital recovery factor that annualizes investment outlays; a scalar

params.name: delta
params.doc: the discount factor applied to each period's cost when forming the present value

params.name: site
params.doc: a location cost multiplier applied to investment outlays at a plant

params.name: size
params.doc: the capacity added by installing one kiln of a given discrete size, in tons per year

params.name: inv_cost
params.doc: the reference investment cost of a productive unit at its reference size

params.name: inv_size
params.doc: the reference size of a productive unit used to scale its investment cost

params.name: inv_scale
params.doc: the scale-economy exponent for a productive unit; together with the size and reference size it gives a constant unit investment cost for each discrete kiln size

params.name: muf
params.doc: the unit cost of shipping finished product from a plant to a market

params.name: muv
params.doc: the unit cost of delivering imported finished product to a market

params.name: mue
params.doc: the unit cost of moving finished product from a plant to the export point

params.name: mui
params.doc: the unit cost of moving an imported intermediate to a plant

params.name: ebu
params.doc: the maximum total finished-product export allowed in a period

params.name: vbu
params.doc: the maximum total finished-product import allowed in a period

params.name: vibu
params.doc: the maximum total clinker import allowed in a period

params.name: pv
params.doc: the import price of a final product or intermediate commodity

params.name: d
params.doc: the required demand for a final product in a market and period, in tons

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the operating level of a process at a plant in a period

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the quantity of a final product shipped from a plant to a market in a period

vars.name: v
vars.doc: the quantity of a final product imported and delivered to a market in a period

vars.name: vi
vars.doc: the quantity of an intermediate commodity imported to a plant in a period

vars.name: e
vars.doc: the quantity of a final product exported from a plant in a period

vars.name: h
vars.doc: the amount of capacity of a productive unit added at a plant in an expansion period, in tons per year

vars.name: y
vars.doc: equals 1 if a kiln of a given discrete size is installed for a scale-economy unit at a plant in an expansion period, and 0 otherwise

vars.name: phi
vars.doc: the total discounted cost over the horizon, which the model minimizes

vars.name: phikap
vars.doc: the capital-investment cost charged in a period

vars.name: phipsi
vars.doc: the recurrent operating cost charged in a period

vars.name: philam
vars.doc: the transport cost charged in a period

vars.name: phipi
vars.doc: the import cost charged in a period

vars.name: phieps
vars.doc: the export revenue earned in a period

vars.name: phiw
vars.doc: the working-capital cost charged in a period

constraints.name: mb
constraints.intent: For each commodity, plant, and period, production must be able to cover what leaves. The amount of the commodity produced by running the processes at the plant, together with any of that commodity imported when it is an intermediate, must be at least the amount of it that is shipped out to markets and exported when it is a final product.

constraints.name: cc
constraints.intent: For each productive unit, plant, and period, the capacity used cannot exceed the capacity available. The capacity consumed by running the processes at the plant must not be more than the existing base capacity of that unit plus all the capacity already added through expansions that are in effect by that period.

constraints.name: id
constraints.intent: For each scale-economy unit, plant, and expansion period, the capacity added must match the kiln chosen. The amount of capacity installed equals the capacity contributed by whichever discrete kiln size is selected in that decision.

constraints.name: ich
constraints.intent: For each scale-economy unit, plant, and expansion period, at most one kiln size may be chosen. The selection across all available sizes can pick at most a single size.

constraints.name: limit
constraints.intent: For each plant, the total capacity that ever exists across the scale-economy units cannot exceed the plant's capacity ceiling. Summing each such unit's existing base capacity and all the capacity it adds over the horizon must stay within the plant's limit.

constraints.name: mr
constraints.intent: For each final product, market, and period, supply must meet demand. The total shipped to the market from all plants plus what is imported to that market must be at least the required demand there.

constraints.name: eb
constraints.intent: For each period, total exports of final products are capped. The sum of all final-product exports from every plant must not exceed the export limit for that period.

constraints.name: vb
constraints.intent: For each period, total imports of final products are capped. The sum of all final-product imports delivered to every market must not exceed the import limit for that period.

constraints.name: vib
constraints.intent: For each period, total clinker imports are capped. The sum of clinker imported to every plant must not exceed the clinker import limit for that period.

constraints.name: obj_def
constraints.intent: The total discounted cost is defined by adding up, over all periods, the discounted sum of that period's investment, operating, transport, import, and working-capital costs less its export revenue.

constraints.name: apsi
constraints.intent: For each period, the recurrent operating cost is defined. It is a small scaling of the combined process operating cost across all plants and the fixed cost of all installed capacity, where installed capacity means each unit's base capacity plus the expansions in effect by that period.

constraints.name: akap
constraints.intent: For each period, the capital-investment cost is defined. It is the capital recovery factor applied to the location-adjusted investment outlays charged in that period: for the scale-economy units, the cost of each chosen kiln size, and for the units without scale economies, the cost proportional to the capacity added, counting only the expansions that are in effect by that period.

constraints.name: alam
constraints.intent: For each period, the transport cost is defined. It is a small scaling of the cost of shipping final products from plants to markets, delivering imported final products to markets, moving exported final products to the export point, and moving imported intermediates to plants.

constraints.name: aeps
constraints.intent: For each period, the export revenue is defined as a small scaling of the total quantity of final products exported from all plants in that period.

constraints.name: api
constraints.intent: For each period, the import cost is defined. It is a small scaling of the cost of imported final products delivered to markets plus the cost of imported intermediates delivered to plants, each valued at its import price.

constraints.name: aw
constraints.intent: For each period, the working-capital cost is defined as a fixed fraction of the sum of that period's recurrent operating cost and its import cost.

query: Two things rub me the wrong way about this plan: we keep sinking money into new dry-kiln after new dry-kiln, and meanwhile we ship nothing abroad at all. Couldn't we just hold the line to a single new dry-kiln across the whole horizon and at the same time push for at least a hundred units of cement exported? Wouldn't a leaner-build, export-oriented program make sense — and what's the model seeing that pulls against running both of those together?
expected_pyomo:
model.limit_dry_kilns = Constraint(expr=sum(model.y['dry-kiln', i, s, te] for i in model.I for s in model.S for te in model.TE) <= 1)
model.export_drive = Constraint(expr=sum(model.e['cement', i, t] for i in model.I for t in model.T) >= 100)
